dropzone.tagline: Check your specification against 30+ USPTO drafting rules
dropzone.title: Drop your patent .docx to begin local analysis
dropzone.subtitle: or click to browse
dropzone.notice: Currently supports U.S. patent specifications (.docx) only.
dropzone.rejectMultiple: Please drop one file at a time.

results.nonPatentWarning: This document may not be a patent specification
results.nonPatentWarningDetails: PatentLint is designed for U.S. patent application drafts. This file does not appear to contain standard patent sections, numbered claims, or paragraph numbering. Analysis results may not be meaningful for non-patent documents.
results.trackedChangesWarning: This document contains tracked changes, which may affect analysis accuracy. Accept or reject all changes in your word processor, then analyze again.

cta.heading: Ready to check your draft?
cta.subtext: Drop a .docx file and get instant results. No upload. No account.

security.airplaneDemoAlt: Demo: analyzing a patent draft in airplane mode with no network connection

badge.headline: No account. No install. No upload.
badge.description: Your file never leaves your device.

prove.step1: Turn off WiFi or enable airplane mode
prove.step3: No internet means no cloud, no upload, no data sent anywhere
prove.step4: After analysis, a green dot on the results page confirms zero network activity.
prove.testHeading: Don't take our word for it
prove.testDescription: This button fetches the PatentLint logo. Watch the log below to see how PatentLint detects network activity.

page.heroTitle: Your Patent Draft Never Leaves Your Device
page.heroSubtext: PatentLint runs entirely inside your browser — no server, no upload, no cloud. Your confidential specification stays on your machine, always.
page.compareDisclaimer: Comparison reflects general architecture patterns. Individual products may vary. "Word Add-in Tools" and "Cloud-Based Tools" are generic categories, not specific products.

q1.question: Does my file leave my device?
q1.addin: File stays local, but add-in may phone home.

q2.question: Do I need an account?
q2.patentlint: No. No sign-up, no login, no tracking.
q2.addin: Usually requires a license or account.

q4.question: Can I verify the privacy claim?
q4.patentlint: Yes. Open DevTools → Network, or go airplane mode.
q4.cloud: No. Data is on their servers.

q5.question: What platforms are supported?
q5.patentlint: Any device with a modern browser.

q6.question: What does it cost?
q6.patentlint: Free — no fees, no subscription, no usage limits.

tech.architecture: PatentLint compiles its Python analysis engine to WebAssembly via Pyodide and runs it entirely inside your browser's sandboxed JavaScript environment. No server-side processing occurs for document analysis.
tech.network: After the initial page load (which loads the analysis engine), zero network requests are made during analysis. Verify this in your browser's DevTools Network tab, or simply turn on airplane mode.
tech.pdf: PDF reports are generated entirely in your browser using pdfmake. Your document content never leaves your device, not even for the report.
tech.ai: PatentLint does not use AI, machine learning, or large language models. All checks are deterministic, rule-based pattern matching against MPEP guidelines. Your text is never sent to any AI service.
tech.telemetry: PatentLint collects zero analytics, zero telemetry, and zero usage data. There are no cookies, no tracking pixels, and no third-party scripts. The application does not know who you are.
tech.selfHosted: For organizations with strict data sovereignty requirements, such as ITAR-controlled environments or air-gapped networks, PatentLint can be self-hosted via Docker with all dependencies included.

about.productDesc1: PatentLint is a free patent specification quality checker. Drop a U.S. patent draft into your browser and get an instant compliance report — no upload, no account, no cloud.
about.productDesc2: It checks drafting rules from the Manual of Patent Examining Procedure (MPEP), catches common issues like missing antecedent basis and restrictive wording, and visualizes your claim dependency tree.
about.usptoSubtitle: USPTO Patent Center validates filing format. PatentLint checks the draft itself.

check.antecedentBasis: Antecedent basis ("the X" without prior "a X")

arch.selfHostBadge: Self-hosted for air-gapped and ITAR-sensitive environments

tip.pyodide: CPython compiled to WebAssembly. Runs Python in the browser
tip.pdfmake: Client-side PDF generation, no server needed
tip.fastapi: Python REST API for server-side deployments
tip.weasyprint: HTML-to-PDF engine with CJK font support

about.builderDesc1: PatentLint was built by a patent engineer who spent years reviewing thousands of patent drafts and catching the same formatting errors and MPEP violations by hand. The goal: automate the tedious parts so practitioners can focus on substance.
about.builderDesc2: The entire analysis engine, written in Python, compiles to WebAssembly and runs directly in your browser. No server, no upload, no files leaving your device. Existing tools either require Windows-only installation or upload documents to cloud servers. PatentLint needs neither.
about.builderSister: Its companion project, the Agentic Patent Analyst, uses LLM agents for prior art search and claim coverage analysis.

details.seeSection112: See § 112 Analysis below for per-claim detail.
details.specSupportUnsupported: {{count}} claim term(s) not found in the specification.

consolidation.nounMismatchSummary: Preamble noun mismatch: {{count}} dependent claim(s) differ from Claim {{rootId}}

restrictiveWording.verify: Restrictive wording found in specification paragraphs.
restrictiveWording.pass: No restrictive wording found in specification.

paragraphEnding.amend: Paragraphs with invalid or missing ending punctuation.
paragraphEnding.pass: All paragraphs have valid ending punctuation.

sequenceListing.amend: SEQ ID NO referenced but no sequence listing statement found.

priorArt.pass: No prior art citations found in background.

trackedChanges.amend: Document contains tracked changes (revisions). Accept or reject all changes before filing.

restrictiveWording.verify: Restrictive or indefinite wording found in claims.
restrictiveWording.pass: No restrictive or indefinite wording found in claims.

missingPeriod.pass: All claims end with a period.

extraPeriod.amend: Claims with extra or misplaced periods.
extraPeriod.pass: No extra periods found in claims.

claims.missingTransition: Claim {{claimNumber}} is missing a transitional phrase (e.g., 'comprising', 'consisting of')
claims.missingTransitionDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} does not contain a recognized transitional phrase. Every claim must include a transitional phrase such as 'comprising', 'consisting of', 'consisting essentially of', 'including', 'containing', 'characterized by', or 'characterized in that' between the preamble and the claim body.

restrictiveWording.verify: Restrictive or improper wording found in abstract.
restrictiveWording.pass: No restrictive or improper wording found in abstract.

structure.amend: Abstract has extra paragraphs or invalid ending.
structure.pass: Abstract is a single paragraph with valid ending.

impliedPhrases.pass: No implied phrases found in abstract.

wordCount.amend: Abstract word count is outside the 50–150 range.
wordCount.pass: Abstract word count is within the 50–150 range.

singleFigure.amend: Single-figure patent uses 'FIG. 1' instead of 'The Figure'.

priorArt.verify: Prior art references found in drawings description.
priorArt.pass: No prior art references in drawings description.

sequential.amend: Figures are not in sequential order.

loading.feature1_title: No Account. No Install. No Upload.
loading.feature1_desc: Your confidential patent drafts never leave your device. Nothing is uploaded, and PatentLint doesn't even know who you are.
loading.feature2_desc: Catches restrictive wording, indefinite terms, and antecedent basis gaps. Visualizes claim dependencies with an interactive tree.
loading.feature3_desc: Interactive dependency tree and Mermaid diagram show your claim structure at a glance.
loading.feature4_desc: Mac, Windows, Linux, iPad: any device with a browser. No Word add-in required. Full UI in English, 繁體中文, 简体中文, and 日本語.
loading.feature5_desc: Drop a .docx file and get results in seconds. Download a PDF report, no cloud round-trip required.

pdf.securityNote: Analyzed on your device -- not uploaded to the cloud.
pdf.disclaimer: Generated by PatentLint. This report does not constitute legal advice. Review by a qualified patent professional is recommended.

checks.preamble_cross_category_mismatch: Cross-category preamble mismatch between dependent and independent claim.
checks.preamble_noun_mismatch: Preamble noun differs between dependent and independent claim.
checks.preamble_indefinite_article: Indefinite article in dependent claim preamble (should be 'The').
checks.spec_support_unsupported_terms: Claim terms not found in specification.
checks.spec_support_pass: All claim terms found in specification.
checks.required_sections_pass: All required sections per MPEP § 608.01(a) are present.
checks.optional_section_missing: Optional section not found. Include if applicable.
checks.figure_xref_pass: All figure references are consistent between Brief Description of Drawings and Detailed Description.
checks.figure_xref_orphaned_brief: Figure(s) described in Brief Description of Drawings but not referenced in Detailed Description.
checks.figure_xref_orphaned_detailed: Figure(s) referenced in Detailed Description but not described in Brief Description of Drawings.

claims.jepsonPriorArt: Claim {{claimNumber}} uses Jepson format — preamble elements are treated as admitted prior art (MPEP § 2129)
claims.jepsonPriorArtDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} is drafted in Jepson format. Under MPEP § 2129, the elements recited in the preamble of a Jepson claim are treated as an implied admission that they are prior art. Verify that this admission is intentional.
claims.crmNonTransitory: Claim {{claimNumber}}: computer-readable medium claim is missing 'non-transitory' qualifier
claims.crmNonTransitoryDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} recites a computer-readable medium without the 'non-transitory' qualifier. Without this qualifier, the claim covers transitory signals (e.g., carrier waves), which are not patent-eligible subject matter under 35 U.S.C. § 101. Add 'non-transitory' before the medium term.
claims.markushOpenTransition: Claim {{claimNumber}}: Markush group uses open-ended '{{transition}}' instead of 'consisting of'
claims.markushOpenTransitionDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} contains a Markush group using '{{transition}}' instead of the required 'consisting of'. Markush groups must use closed transitional language per MPEP § 2117. Using open-ended language may result in an improper Markush grouping rejection.
claims.omnibusClaim: Claim {{claimNumber}} appears to be an omnibus claim
claims.omnibusClaimDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} references the description or drawings without reciting specific technical features. Omnibus claims are categorically rejected under 35 U.S.C. § 112(b) in U.S. utility patents (MPEP § 2173.05(r)). Rewrite the claim to recite specific structural or method limitations.
claims.specialFormatsPass: No special claim format issues detected.
claims.missingPeriod: Claim {{claimNumber}} does not end with a period.
claims.missingPeriodDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} is missing its final period. Every claim must end with a single period per MPEP § 608.01(m).
claims.extraPeriod: Claim {{claimNumber}} contains extra or misplaced periods.
claims.extraPeriodDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} has periods in unexpected positions. A claim should contain only one period at the very end per MPEP § 608.01(m).
claims.whereinComma: Claim {{claimNumber}}: review comma usage before 'wherein' clause.
claims.whereinCommaDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} may have incorrect comma placement around a 'wherein' clause. Review punctuation per MPEP § 608.01(m).
